channel.title: Middle East on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Middle East on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Depression would be cost-effectively decreased in teens by Vitamin D (Iran)
item.description:
• Is vitamin D supplementation program in Iranian schools effective in reducing adolescent depressive symptoms? Cost effectiveness study

Is vitamin D supplementation program in Iranian schools effective in reducing adolescent depressive symptoms? Cost effectiveness study

BMC Public Health . 2023 Jul 20;23(1):1393. doi: 10.1186/s12889-023-16244-z. PDF behind paywall

Seyed Vahid Jasemi 1, Zhale Zandieh 2, Narges Zandieh 1, Mohsen Rezaei Hemami 3, Ali Darvishi 4 5, Zahra Abdollahi 6, Ramin Heshmat 7

Purpose: We aimed to assess the cost-effectiveness of the vitamin D supplementation program in Iranian adolescents reducing adolescent depressive Symptoms.

item.title: Infertile women had less Vitamin D (Baghdad in this case)
item.description:
• Prevalence of 25-Hydroxyvitamin D (Vitamin D) Deficiency in a Group of Infertile Women from Baghdad City

Prevalence of 25-Hydroxyvitamin D (Vitamin D) Deficiency in a Group of Infertile Women from Baghdad City

Biochemistry Research International Vol 2023 https://doi.org/10.1155/2023/6597730

Muthana Anad Majid ,1 Wafaa Nasser Hassan ,2 a nd Amna Fadhil Ridha 3

Chart of data made by VitaminDWiki

Background. Infertility is a common issue affecting a large number of Iraqi women of reproductive age. The relationship between vitamin D deficiency and infertility has previously drawn the attention of gynecologists, and an increasing number of vitamin D testing has been requested.

item.title: Mortality reduced if Vitamin D levels raised to 30 ng ( Middle East this time) - Grant
item.description:
• Reducing incidence and mortality rates in the UAE and related countries by raising serum 25(OH)D above 30 ng/mL

Reducing incidence and mortality rates in the UAE and related countries by raising serum 25(OH)D above 30 ng/mL

Presented at Vitamin D Conference – 9th Abu Dhabi – Oct 2022

Note by VitaminDWiki: #7 for Saudi Arabia needs Magnesium (none in de-salinated water)

Download the entire PDF from VitaminDWiki

item.title: Vitamin D Conference – 9th Abu Dhabi
item.description:
Conference Brochure as of Oct 8 - on VitaminDWiki

Virtual Conference, Scientific Agenda Oct 7-8 2022  URL

Cost for non-doctors 400 UAE = $110

Note; the following list is from the original, not updated brochure

  • KEYNOTE: Vitamin D and Immunity: Current State of the Field - Prof. Dr. John H. White

  • The role of Vitamin D in respiratory infection - Prof. Peter Kvietys

  • The Relation between Self-Tolerance and Vitamin D through Regulatory T cells (Tregs) Prof. Peter Kvietys Dr. Gianina Elena Statache

item.title: Low vitamin D is big problem during pregnancies (Saudi Arabia in this case)
item.description:
• Association between different levels of maternal vitamin-D status during pregnancy and maternal outcomes

Association between different levels of maternal vitamin-D status during pregnancy and maternal outcomes

Clin Nutr ESPEN . 2022 Aug;50:307-313. doi: 10.1016/j.clnesp.2022.04.024

Mona Alanazi 1, Reda M Nabil Aboushady 2, Amel Dawod Kamel 3

Background and aims: Maternal vitamin-D deficiency is a significant health concern among pregnant women in the Middle East. Vitamin-D deficiency during pregnancy has been associated with adverse maternal outcomes and an increased risk of pregnancy complications. This study aimed to identify the level of vitamin-D during pregnancy and examine the effect of different vitamin-D levels during pregnancy on maternal outcomes.

item.title: COVID 3X more likely if a poor Receptor (cells get less Vitamin D from the blood)
item.description:
• VDR gene polymorphisms are associated with the increased susceptibility to COVID-19 among iranian population: A case-control study
      • References

VDR gene polymorphisms are associated with the increased susceptibility to COVID-19 among iranian population: A case-control study

Int J Immunogenet. 2022 Jul 21. doi: 10.1111/iji.12585   PDF costs $10 to rent

Ali Jafarpoor 1, Seyed Mohammad Jazayeri 1 2, Farah Bokharaei-Salim 3, Angila Ataei-Pirkooh 3, Azam Ghaziasadi 1 2, Saber Soltani 1, Ahmadreza Sadeghi 1 4, Shima Sadeghipoor Marvi 1, Vahdat Poortahmasebi 1 5, Seyed Mahmood Seyed Khorrami 6, Mandana Hasanzad 6 7, Negar Parsania 6, Sina Nagozir 6, Narges Mokhtari 6, Ali Parsania 6, Asma Bahrami 6, Mohammad Hossein Nadjarha 6, Reza Pakzad 8, Masoud Parsania 1 9

item.title: Poor sleep associated with low Vitamin D (pregnant Saudis in the case)
item.description:
• Changes in Sleep Patterns during Pregnancy and Predictive Factors: A Longitudinal Study in Saudi Women

Changes in Sleep Patterns during Pregnancy and Predictive Factors: A Longitudinal Study in Saudi Women

Nutrients. 2022 Jun 25;14(13):2633. doi: 10.3390/nu14132633.

Sara Al-Musharaf 1

This study aimed to assess sleep patterns during the three trimesters of pregnancy and whether vitamin D concentrations, along with other risk factors, are associated with these alterations. In a longitudinal study, 140 pregnant women (age 18 to 39 years) were followed throughout their first, second, and third trimesters. Sleep was measured using the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) at each trimester, along with an assessment of biochemical parameters, including serum vitamin D levels. The information that was collected included anthropometric data, socio-economic status, dietary intake, and physical activity. The PSQI was higher in mid and late pregnancy than in early pregnancy (both p = 0.001), and the sleep duration was also higher in late versus early pregnancy.

item.title: Vitamin D helps some child health problems in Pakistan
item.description:
• Adjunctive vitamin D therapy in various diseases in children: a scenario according to standard guideline

Adjunctive vitamin D therapy in various diseases in children: a scenario according to standard guideline

BMC Pediatrics volume 22, https://doi.org/10.1186/s12887-022-03297-z

Hafsa Arshad, Faiz Ullah Khan, Naveed Ahmed, Naveed Anwer, Ali Hassan Gillani & Asim.ur. Rehman

Strange, no enties between 1,000 IU to 200,000 IU

Background

Adherence to standard guidelines is imperative when question comes to disease management. The present study aimed to evaluate the administration of adjunctive vitamin D therapy in various diseases, its adherence to standard guideline and the effect of socioeconomic status on the consumption of vitamin D in children.

item.title: Long-Haul COVID after 3 months – only 5 percent had even 30 ng of Vitamin D
item.description:
• Prevalence of vitamin D deficiency among patients attending Post COVID-19 follow-up clinic: a cross-sectional study
      • Opinion: Long-Haul needs both high dose vitamin D and re-activation of the Vitamin D Receptor
      • Females in Egypt were more likely to get Long-Haul
      • Vitamin D: A Role Also in Long COVID-19? - April 2022

Prevalence of vitamin D deficiency among patients attending Post COVID-19 follow-up clinic: a cross-sectional study

Eur Rev Med Pharmacol Sci . 2022 Apr;26(8):3038-3045. doi: 10.26355/eurrev_202204_28635.

item.title: Diabetes prevented by 50,000 IU vitamin D monthly (Iran)
item.description:
• Economic evaluation of a national vitamin D supplementation program among Iranian adolescents for the prevention of adulthood type 2 diabetes mellitus
      • VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D Proven to fight 93 health problems
      • VitaminDWiki - Weekly, Monthly Vitamin D are typically better than daily - many studies

Economic evaluation of a national vitamin D supplementation program among Iranian adolescents for the prevention of adulthood type 2 diabetes mellitus

BMC Complement Med Ther. 2022 Jan 3;22(1):1. doi: 10.1186/s12906-021-03474-0.

item.title: Infants have gotten free 400 IU of vitamin D in Turkey since 2005, More for longer would be even better
item.description:
• The Effect of Vitamin D Prophylaxis on 25-OH Vitamin D Levels in Children

The Effect of Vitamin D Prophylaxis on 25-OH Vitamin D Levels in Children

Turk Arch Pediatr. 2021 Nov;56(6):618-623. doi: 10.5152/TurkArchPediatr.2021.21166.

İhsan Gül 1, Emel Gür 2, Tuğba Erener Ercan 3, Günay Can 4

Background: Vitamin D deficiency is a major public health problem. The aim of our study was to determine serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels among healthy children aged 3-36 months in a setting where vitamin D prophylaxis is a national policy for infants during the first year of life and among pregnant women.

item.title: 400 IU of vitamin D helped a few adults (Orthodox Jewish mothers - heavy clothing) – 2001
item.description:
Cannot recall a single other study that found 400 IU for an adult could be even detected

      • Vitamin D Deficiency and Insufficiency in Orthodox and Non-Orthodox Jewish Mothers in Israel

Vitamin D Deficiency and Insufficiency in Orthodox and Non-Orthodox Jewish Mothers in Israel

IMAJ 2001,3:419-421

Menashe N. Mukamel MD1,2, Yosef Weisman MD1,2, Raz Somech MD2, Zipora Eisenberg MSc1, Jacob Landman MD4, Itzhak Shapira MD1, Zvi Spirer MD2,3 and Uri Jurgenson MD2

item.title: Two thirds of South Asians have low Vitamin D (less than 20 ng) – meta-analysis
item.description:
• High prevalence of vitamin D deficiency among the South Asian adults:

High prevalence of vitamin D deficiency among the South Asian adults:

a systematic review and meta-analysis

BMC Public Health. 2021 Oct 9;21(1):1823. doi: 10.1186/s12889-021-11888-1.

Mahbubul H Siddiqee # 1 2, Badhan Bhattacharjee # 3 4, Umme Ruman Siddiqi 5, Mohammad MeshbahurRahman 6

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Background: Vitamin-D deficiency is linked to a wide range of chronic and infectious diseases. Body of literature suggested that the prevalence of this deficiency can have geographical variation. Although vitamin D deficiency is frequently reported in the South Asian population, the scarcity of systematic reviews and meta-analysis means the true extent of the disease and the underlying factors causing it are poorly characterized.

item.title: Very low Vitamin D in Indian pregnancies– 1 in 3 women not even have 20 ng – meta-analysis
item.description:
Pooled estimate of vitamin D deficiency among pregnant women in India: a systematic review and meta-analysis

J Health Popul Nutr. 2021 Jun 29;40(1):28. doi: 10.1186/s41043-021-00253-y.

Angeline Jeyakumar 1 2, Vidhya Shinde 3, Reshma Ravindran 3

Background: Vitamin D deficiency among pregnant women is a public health concern globally. In India, individual studies report high prevalence. However, lack of national data masks the true burden. This work determined the pooled prevalence of vitamin D deficiency among pregnant women in India through a systematic review of literature and meta-analysis.

item.title: Vitamin D levels increased in Saudi Arabia over a decade (more sun or supplements)
2.5 X more health compaints in children and youths if low vitamin D
Vitamin D has eliminated ICU COVID-19 in hospital in Dubai since June
Hospital COVID-19 observation: 7X more likely to live if more than 20 ng of vitamin D
60,000 IU dose of VItamin D (weekly, monthly) is popular in India
item.description:
Available as both powder (added to food or drinks) and capsules

  • AIWO Amazon Nov 2020

  • CADILA (Vitamin D3-Cholecalciferol) 60000 IU, Pack of 5 x 4 Capsules, Once a Week Amazon India Nov 2020

  • Appears to be 8 cents per capsule

    • Amazon India does not ship to US but This does I placed an order Nov 21

  • Vitamin D Supplements in the Indian Market - 2016   PDF

Dosing Recommendations by Sharan

  • 0- 10 ng/ml:- 60,000 IU twice a week for 4 weeks followed by once a week for 6 weeks for levels to reach above 30 ng/ml

item.title: COVID-19 increased 3.5 X if Ultra-Orthodox (cloth) Sept, 2020
item.description:
The link between vitamin D deficiency and Covid-19 in a large population – preprint Sept 7

Deficiency = 12 ng

Based on Vitamin D tests were made sometime in the previous 10 years

82% of Arab females had < 20 ng

1.8 X higher risk if <12 ng vs >30 ng - independent of sex, race

Study does not appear to have information on vitamin D levels and

  • infection severity (ICU, death, etc)

item.title: Vitamin D genes vary with UVB in Europe, Africa, Asia
2,000 IU vitamin D is the smallest dose in Saudi Arabia (GCC statement)
item.description:
Diagnosis and Management of Vitamin D Deficiency in the Gulf Cooperative Council (GCC) Countries: An Expert Consensus Summary Statement From the GCC Vitamin D Advisory Board

Arch Osteoporos, 15 (1), 35 2020 Mar 2, DOI: 10.1007/s11657-020-0709-8

Other Gulf Cooperation Council countries have smaller dose sizes

item.title: Middle Eastern women living in Sweden 23X higher risk to be anxious if low vitamin D
Obese taking 50,000 IU of vitamin D weekly lost more weight (again) – RCT
Turkey needs to give Vitamin D to children too (infants get D for free)
item.description:
Suspected by the founder of VitaminDWiki

Suspect a bimodal distribution of vitamin D levels:

  1. Higher levels if child got Vitamin D from parents - carr-on from free govt. Vitamin D for infants

  2. Lower levels if no Vitamin D supplementation

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Objective:

This study aimed to determine the ratio of seasonal vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency in elementary school children aged between 6-9 years old, living in one of the largest metropols of Europe, İstanbul.

item.title: Miraculous Physiological Activities of Vitamin D (30 to 100 ng)
item.description:
MIRACULOUS PHYSIOLOGICAL ACTIVITIES OF VITAMIN D AND ITS ROLE IN HUMAN HEALTH AND DISEASES

  • Vitamin D levels from lowest to highest: Middle East, China. India, S EU. N EU – Dec 2019

    • 56% in India have < 20-ng

  • Children in India – 1 in 7 extremely low Vitamin D, 1 in 10 prediabetic – Sept 2019

  • International Conference of Vitamin D Fortification of Food (India) – Sept 2018

item.title: Vitamin D levels from lowest to highest: Middle East, China. India, S EU. N EU
item.description:
Overview Deficiency of vitamin D has the following

  • Vitamin D levels around the world - DSM review June 2014 which has the following

item.title: Vitamin D added to cooking oil increased D by 9 ng improved health (Iran)
item.description:
Vitamin D-fortified cooking oil is an effective way to improve vitamin D status: an institutional efficacy trial

Eur J Nutr (2019). https://doi.org/10.1007/s00394-019-02103-4

Nikooyeh, B., Zargaraan, A., Kalayi, A. et al

60 lunches (3 months) at two factories in Iran

30 grams of sunflower cooking oil per person– add Vitamin D:

    ~500 IU (after cooking) or 0 IU (at a location 40 km away)

Raised Vitamin D levels by 9 ng/ml in 3 months

item.title: Children in India – 1 in 7 extremely low Vitamin D, 1 in 10 prediabetic
item.description:
Diabetes Risk In Children On A Rise, Finds Nutrition Survey; Symptoms Of Diabetes In Children NDTV

1% of school-age children and adolescents were diabetic

3% of school-age children and 4% of adolescents had high total cholesterol.

7% of school-age children and adolescents were at risk for chronic kidney disease.

5% of adolescents were classified as having hypertension

5% of children under five were stunted,

22 % of school-age children were stunted

item.title: Women wearing hijab in tropics need at least 1 hour noon sun daily in summer
item.description:
Optimizing ultraviolet B radiation exposure to prevent vitamin D deficiency among pregnant women in the tropical zone: report from cohort study on vitamin D status and its impact during pregnancy in Indonesia.

BMC Pregnancy Childbirth. 2019 Jun 21;19(1):209. doi: 10.1186/s12884-019-2306-7.

Judistiani RTD1,2, Nirmala SA3,4, Rahmawati M5, Ghrahani R4,6,7, Natalia YA3, Sugianli AK7,8, Indrati AR4,7,8, Suwarsa O4,7,9, Setiabudiawan B4,6,7.

#### Notes:

  1. This study appears to incorrectly assume that 30 ng of vitamin D is enough

item.title: Vitamin D Receptor, the gut, the immune system, and the Middle East
Need 1 hour of noon sun to get Hijab women to 20 ng of Vitamin D (cloth)
item.description:
Several studies on this page

      • Optimizing ultraviolet B radiation exposure to prevent vitamin D deficiency among pregnant women in the tropical zone: report from cohort study on vitamin D status and its impact during pregnancy in Indonesia.
      • Some alternate solutions on VitaminDWiki
      • The Effect of Clothing on Vitamin D Status, Bone Turnover Markers, and Bone Mineral Density in Young Kuwaiti Females - June 2019

Optimizing ultraviolet B radiation exposure to prevent vitamin D deficiency among pregnant women in the tropical zone: report from cohort study on vitamin D status and its impact during pregnancy in Indonesia.

BMC Pregnancy Childbirth. 2019 Jun 21;19(1):209. doi: 10.1186/s12884-019-2306-7.

item.title: Summer SAD (Seasonal Affective Disorder) – Middle East
item.description:
As the mercury rises, make sure you don’t get summer SAD National – UAE

“Staying out of the sun completely can leave us deficient in vitamin D and prone to depressive symptoms”

  • “Summer SAD is associated with insomnia, agitation, reduced appetite and weight loss”

Refers to his 2011 publication: Sunshine, Sadness and Seasonality

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Overview Middle East and vitamin D starts with

The, ahem, ‘Joy’ of Summer in Dubai

item.title: Vitamin D level of outdoor workers in India was only 18 ng
item.description:
Absence of vitamin D deficiency among common outdoor workers in Delhi.

Clin Endocrinol (Oxf). 2019 May 14. doi: 10.1111/cen.14012.

Dharmshaktu P1, Saha S1, Kar P1, Sreenivas V1, Ramakrishnan L1, Goswami R1.

1 Dept of Endocrinology and Metabolism, Cardiac Biochemistry and Biostatistics, All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi, 110029.

  • International Conference of Vitamin D Fortification of Food (India) – Sept 2018

item.title: Iranian low vitamin D levels have been increasing
International Conference on Vitamin D Deficiency – 8th Abu Dhabi
item.description:
Schedule for April 12 2019

“The World’s largest conference on Vitamin D”

  1. Vitamin D and Adolescents’ Health

  2. Low Protein Diet and Vitamin D Supplements in Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) Patients

  3. Vitamin D and Liver Diseases: Update and Overview

  4. New Insights into the Role of Vitamin D in the Immune System

  5. Vitamin D Deficiency Rickets in Children: Not a Disease from the Past

  6. Difficult Diagnoses in Rickets

  7. Vitamin D Deficiency in Riyadh: A Decade of an Epidemic (2008-2018)

item.title: 450,000 IU of vitamin D over 9 weeks given to 100,000 teenage Iranian girls helped their livers
item.description:
High dose vitamin D supplementation is associated with an improvement in serum markers of liver function

BioFactors https://doi.org/10.1002/biof.1496

Hamidreza Tavakoli Hossein Rostami Amir Avan Mohammad Bagherniya Gordon A. Ferns

Sayyed Saeid Khayyatzadeh khayyatzadeh@yahoo.com, Majid Ghayour‐Mobarhan ghayourmobarhan@yahoo.com;

This appears to be the first study of the affects of high-dose vitamin D given to 100,000 Iranian girls. In early 2015

9.4 ng of Vitamin D+ 9 weeks of 50,000 IU/week ==> 36.4 ng

What other studies will be published on this massive intervention?

item.title: 85 ng of vitamin D in soldiers deployed to sunny Afghanistan
item.description:
Serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D fluctuations in military personnel during 6-month summer operational deployments in Afghanistan.

Br J Nutr. 2019 Jan 3:1-9. doi: 10.1017/S000711451800346X.

Fallowfield JL1, Delves SK1, Hill NE2, Lanham-New SA3, Shaw AM1, Brown PEH1, Bentley C4, Wilson DR2, Allsopp AJ1.

1 Applied Physiology Department,Institute of Naval Medicine,Alverstoke,Hampshire PO12 2DL,UK.

2 Royal Centre for Defence Medicine,Birmingham B15 2SQ,UK.

3 Nutritional Sciences Dept,Faculty of Health and Medical Sciences,School of Biosciences and Medicine,U. of Surrey,Guildford,SurreyGU2 7XH,UK.

item.title: Swedish TV Warns 'Dark-Skinned' People, Muslims of Vitamin D Deficiency
item.description:
News Item

“A 2015 study in Borlänge, Sahra’s home town, found that 9 out of 10 pregnant Somali women in Sweden suffer from severe vitamin D deficiency, which, coupled with other risk factors, can contribute to a child developing autism. Incidentally, this diagnosis is three to four times more common among children with Somali background, "

“The number of Muslims living in Sweden has been estimated at around 800,000, or roughly 8 percent of Sweden’s population of about 10 million”

item.title: All preteen aged girls in India taking 2,000 IU of vitamin D got levels above 20 ng – RCT
item.description:
• Supplementation with Three Different Daily Doses of Vitamin D3 in Healthy Pre-pubertal School Girls: A Cluster Randomized Trial Nov 2018
      • Most deficient schoolchildren got > 20 ng with 2,000 IU daily - RCT March 2019

Supplementation with Three Different Daily Doses of Vitamin D3 in Healthy Pre-pubertal School Girls: A Cluster Randomized Trial Nov 2018

Indian Pediatrics, Nov 2018, Vol 55,Issue 11, pp 951–956 https://doi.org/10.1007/s13312-018-1416-z

RAMAN KUMAR MARWAHA1, A MITHAL2, NEETU BHARI3, G SETHURAMAN3, SUSHIL GUPTA4, MANOJ SHUKLA4,
